name: SEO Insights MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server providing SEO analysis capabilities to AI agents. Enables agents to analyze web pages for SEO factors — meta tags, heading structure, page speed, mobile-friendliness, structured data, canonical URLs, and technical SEO issues — helping AI-assisted digital marketing and web optimization workflows.
use_cases: Analyzing web page SEO factors from content optimization agents
Auditing technical SEO issues across website pages from marketing agents
Checking page speed and mobile optimization scores from web performance agents
Comparing competitor SEO patterns from digital marketing research agents
not_for: Keyword rank tracking (requires dedicated rank tracking tools)
Backlink analysis (requires specialized backlink APIs like Ahrefs/Moz)
Real-time traffic monitoring (use analytics platforms for that)
best_when: A digital marketer, SEO specialist, or developer wants AI agents to analyze on-page SEO factors — technical SEO auditing, meta analysis, and page optimization recommendations.
avoid_when: You need backlink data, rank tracking, or traffic analytics. This focuses on on-page and technical SEO analysis.

auth.notes: No authentication required for public page analysis. Google PageSpeed API key may improve rate limits.

pricing.notes: Free open source tool. External API calls (Google PageSpeed, etc.) are free within rate limits.

performance.rate_limits: Bounded by external API rate limits (Google PageSpeed: 25k requests/day free)

known_agent_gotchas: External page fetching may trigger bot detection on some sites
PageSpeed data reflects current page state — caching may affect results
SEO analysis of competitor pages is generally legal but check site ToS
External API rate limits (Google PageSpeed) may throttle bulk analysis
agent_readiness.security_notes: External page fetching for analysis. HTTPS to analyzed pages. No credentials required. Public pages only — respect robots.txt.

security_score_components.security_notes: Public page analysis. Respect robots.txt. External HTTPS requests. No credentials.
